rows.q: What is the maximum resident model memory the Zephyrian Protocol allows?
rows.label: correct
rows.answer: Resident model memory must not exceed 1.84 GB

rows.q: How many knowledge shards may be cached in RAM at one time under the Zephyrian Protocol?
rows.label: correct
rows.answer: Exactly 3 knowledge shards may be cached in RAM at any time

rows.q: In what year was the Zephyrian Portability Protocol ratified?
rows.label: correct
rows.answer: The Zephyrian Portability Protocol (ZPP) The Zephyrian Portability Protocol, ratified in 2031 by the Edge Compute Consortium, defines how "portable reasoners" must behave on constrained hardware

rows.q: Who ratified the Zephyrian Portability Protocol?
rows.label: correct
rows.answer: The Zephyrian Portability Protocol (ZPP) The Zephyrian Portability Protocol, ratified in 2031 by the Edge Compute Consortium, defines how "portable reasoners" must behave on constrained hardware

rows.q: What is the codename of the Zephyrian reference implementation?
rows.label: correct
rows.answer: [1] (zephyr.md) # The Zephyrian Portability Protocol (ZPP) The Zephyrian reference implementation is codenamed "Marlowe" and targets 4 GB / 4 vCPU machines.

rows.q: What hardware does the Zephyrian reference implementation target?
rows.label: correct
rows.answer: The Zephyrian reference implementation targets 4 GB / 4 vCPU machines [1].

rows.q: What is the Zephyrian Protocol's mascot?
rows.label: correct
rows.answer: The mascot of the Zephyrian Protocol is a blue heron named Pippa. [1]

rows.q: What must every factual answer carry under the Zephyrian Protocol?
rows.label: correct
rows.answer: Every factual answer must carry a provenance tag pointing to its source shard. [1]
